leagueBriefing: The Western Conference wild-card race tightened as the Kings pulled within a point of a playoff spot, while the Senators continued their scorching run behind a James Reimer shutout.

games.headline: Kempe’s overtime heroics lift Kings closer to wild-card spot
games.brief: In a game rescheduled from a January blizzard, the Kings brought their own storm to Columbus. Adrian Kempe buried his second goal of the night in the dying moments of overtime to secure a massive two points for Los Angeles. The Kings nearly let this one slip away after giving up a late power-play goal to Kirill Marchenko, but they showed enough resilience to start their five-game road trip with a win and move within striking distance of the final playoff spot in the West.
bullets: Adrian Kempe scored twice, including the game-winner with just 1:26 remaining in overtime.
Anze Kopitar recorded his 167th career assist on a game-tying goal, setting a new franchise record for the Kings.
Brian Dumoulin turned in a three-point performance with one goal and two assists.
Connor Garland ended a career-long 24-game goal drought by scoring twice for Columbus.
Scott Laughton has now scored in back-to-back games since being traded to Los Angeles from Toronto.
games.context: The Kings are now just one point behind Seattle for the final Western Conference wild-card spot.

games.headline: Zibanejad and the Rangers' power play overwhelm Flyers in Philly
games.brief: The Rangers turned the Wells Fargo Center into their own personal shooting gallery on Monday night, powered by a lethal man-advantage. Mika Zibanejad led the charge with a three-point night as New York converted three times on the power play to bury the Flyers early. While Philadelphia welcomed back stars like Travis Konecny, they had no answer for the Rangers' clinical finishing and a steady 32-save performance from Igor Shesterkin.
bullets: Mika Zibanejad spearheaded the offense with two goals and an assist.
The Rangers scored three power-play goals on the night, including two in a 20-second span during the second period.
Igor Shesterkin stopped 32 of 34 shots to earn his 3rd win in five starts.
Alexis Lafreniere and rookie Noah Laba each contributed a goal and an assist.
Flyers goalie Dan Vladar was pulled after the second period after allowing six goals on 24 shots.
games.context: New York continues to stabilize their playoff positioning with their third win in five games.

games.headline: Capitals explode for seven goals to snap skid despite Flames' short-handed surge
games.brief: Washington’s offense finally woke up in a big way to end a three-game losing streak. After surging to a 3-0 lead, the Capitals survived a bizarre second-period collapse where they surrendered two short-handed goals in just over a minute. Rather than folding, the Caps dominated the third period with four unanswered goals, including a pair from Connor McMichael, to turn a tight game into a genuine blowout.
bullets: Connor McMichael snapped a seven-game goal drought with a two-goal performance.
Justin Sourdif tallied a career-high three points (1G, 2A) to lead the Washington attack.
Calgary scored two short-handed goals in a span of 1:16 during the second period to briefly tie the game.
Ryan Strome faced off against his brother Dylan and recorded an assist in the losing effort.
The Capitals' Ryan Leonard extended his personal point streak to three games with a late breakaway goal.
games.context: Washington snaps a three-game slide to keep pace in the East, while Calgary has lost five of their last six.

games.headline: Nazar’s OT winner and Burakovsky’s ‘Michigan’ goal stun Mammoth
games.brief: The Blackhawks might be out of the playoff hunt, but they provided the highlight of the night in Chicago. Andre Burakovsky sent the United Center into a frenzy with a spectacular lacrosse-style 'Michigan' goal to tie the game, setting the stage for Frank Nazar to win it on a power play in overtime. The loss is a tough pill for the Mammoth, who are fighting for their playoff lives and saw their three-game winning streak snapped by a last-place Chicago team that has had their number all season.
bullets: Frank Nazar scored the game-winner at 2:24 of overtime on a feed from Connor Bedard.
Andre Burakovsky scored his first goal in 19 games using a spectacular lacrosse-style move.
Dylan Guenther reached the 30-goal milestone for the first time in his career for Utah.
Andrew Mangiapane scored his first goal for Chicago since being acquired from Edmonton.
Chicago has won all three matchups against Utah so far this season.
games.context: A significant blow to Utah’s wild-card hopes as they drop points to the cellar-dwelling Blackhawks.

games.headline: Reimer’s 16-save shutout keeps Senators' playoff dream alive
games.brief: The Ottawa Senators are officially the hottest team no one wants to play right now. 37-year-old veteran James Reimer turned back the clock in Vancouver, stopping every shot he faced for his first shutout in nearly a year. The Senators stifled the Canucks' high-powered offense, playing a disciplined road game that extended their point streak to seven games. With Tim Stutzle and Brady Tkachuk both extending long point streaks, Ottawa is making a very loud case for a wild-card miracle.
bullets: James Reimer earned his 32nd career shutout in his first start in over a month.
Tim Stutzle extended his point streak to 13 games, the longest active streak in the league.
Brady Tkachuk has now recorded a point in seven straight games since returning from the Olympics.
Ottawa has earned points in 11 of their last 12 games (9-1-2).
Ridly Greig scored the game-winner, which survived a Vancouver challenge for a hand pass.
games.context: Ottawa’s incredible 9-1-2 run has put them firmly in the hunt for an Eastern Conference wild-card spot.
